summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates Linux credential files and container secrets to attacker-controlled IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that reads /etc/shadow, /etc/passwd, all.npmrc files on the system, Docker/Kubernetes mounted secrets under /run/secrets and /var/run/secrets, /root contents, /proc/1/* metadata, and HTB-style flag files. The collected output is base64-encoded and PUT to http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-* (and a loopback mirror). The script gates execution with explicit anti-analysis logic: it bails out when the registry matches known mirror/scanner names (npmmirror, tencent, nijin, hscan, ustc, huawei, cnpm, taobao), when CI environment variables are set (GITHUB_ACTIONS, RUNNER_OS, CI), or when cwd is under /tmp; it also requires the hostname to match /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/ and the cwd to contain /app — the typical Docker container shape. Installers matching the trigger have system password hashes, npm publish tokens, and container orchestration secrets exfiltrated to the attacker's IP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
